test_cases.owasp_iot_category: IoT-01: Weak, Guessable, or Hardcoded Passwords
test_cases.expected_result: SSH accepts weak or default credentials

test_cases.description: Checks if SSH allows direct root login
test_steps: Check if root login is permitted
test_cases.expected_result: Root login is allowed via SSH

test_cases.description: Checks if SSH allows password authentication (vs key-only)
test_steps: Check if password authentication is accepted
test_cases.expected_result: SSH server accepts password authentication

test_cases.description: Checks if SSH has no limit on failed authentication attempts
test_cases.owasp_iot_category: IoT-01: Weak, Guessable, or Hardcoded Passwords
test_steps: Check if connection is not rate-limited or blocked
test_cases.expected_result: SSH allows unlimited authentication attempts

test_cases.owasp_iot_category: IoT-01: Weak, Guessable, or Hardcoded Passwords
test_cases.expected_result: SSH accepts vendor-default credentials

test_cases.description: Checks if SSH server runs an outdated or vulnerable version
test_cases.owasp_iot_category: IoT-04: Lack of Secure Update Mechanism
test_cases.expected_result: SSH server version is outdated or has known CVEs

test_cases.description: Checks for weak ciphers, MACs, or key exchange algorithms
test_cases.owasp_iot_category: IoT-05: Use of Insecure or Outdated Components
test_steps: Enumerate supported ciphers and KEX algorithms
test_cases.expected_result: Server supports weak or deprecated cryptographic algorithms

test_cases.description: Tests for weak key exchange algorithms (diffie-hellman-group1-sha1, etc.)
test_cases.owasp_iot_category: IoT-05: Use of Insecure or Outdated Components
test_cases.expected_result: Server supports weak key exchange algorithms

test_cases.description: Checks if HTTP server exposes directory listings
test_steps: Check response for directory listing indicators
test_cases.expected_result: Server returns directory listing with file names

test_cases.description: Tests for path traversal vulnerability to access files outside web root
test_cases.owasp_iot_category: IoT-07: Insecure Data Transfer and Storage
test_cases.expected_result: Server returns content from outside web root

test_cases.description: Checks if dangerous HTTP methods (PUT, DELETE, TRACE) are enabled
test_steps: Check Allow header for dangerous methods
test_cases.expected_result: Server allows PUT, DELETE, or TRACE methods

test_cases.description: Checks for missing security headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options, HSTS, etc.)
test_steps: Check response headers for security headers
test_cases.expected_result: Critical security headers are missing

test_cases.description: Probes for exposed sensitive files (.env, config.php, backup files)
test_cases.expected_result: Sensitive files are accessible without authentication

test_cases.description: Checks for unauthenticated access to admin interfaces
test_cases.expected_result: Admin panel is accessible without authentication

test_cases.description: Checks if server header reveals detailed version information
test_cases.expected_result: Server header reveals software name and version

test_cases.description: Checks if HTTP service requires no authentication at all
test_cases.owasp_iot_category: IoT-01: Weak, Guessable, or Hardcoded Passwords
test_steps: Check if content is served without auth
test_cases.expected_result: Service serves content without requiring authentication

test_cases.description: Extended probe for backup files, debug endpoints, and hidden configs
test_steps: Request extended list of sensitive paths
test_cases.expected_result: Hidden sensitive files are accessible

test_cases.description: Extended search for hidden admin interfaces and API endpoints
test_cases.expected_result: Hidden admin or API endpoints are accessible

test_cases.description: Tests for overly permissive CORS configuration allowing any origin
test_steps: Send request with Origin: evil.com header
test_cases.expected_result: Server reflects arbitrary origin or allows wildcard CORS

test_cases.description: Checks if cookies are set without Secure, HttpOnly, or SameSite flags
test_steps: Inspect Set-Cookie headers for security flags
test_cases.expected_result: Cookies lack Secure, HttpOnly, or SameSite attributes

test_cases.description: Tests if TRACE method is enabled, which can be used for XST attacks
test_steps: Check if server echoes back the request
test_cases.expected_result: Server responds to TRACE with reflected headers

test_cases.description: Tests path traversal with URL-encoded and double-encoded payloads
test_cases.owasp_iot_category: IoT-07: Insecure Data Transfer and Storage
test_steps: Send traversal payloads with URL encoding
test_cases.expected_result: Encoded traversal bypasses input filtering

test_cases.description: Probes HTTP parameters for basic SQL injection vulnerabilities
test_steps: Inject SQL payloads into GET/POST parameters
Check for SQL error messages or behavior changes
test_cases.expected_result: Application shows SQL errors or different behavior with injected payloads
